name: ripgrep (Fast Code Search)

performance.notes: Rust-based; 5-10x faster than grep on large codebases; SIMD-accelerated

agent_readiness.notes: Code search tool replacing grep. Respects .gitignore. Unicode-aware. -t/--type for file types. -g glob patterns. -i case insensitive. --multiline. -A/-B/-C context lines. -l/--files-with-matches. --json for machine-readable output. Default for many editors (VS Code, Neovim). rg command. Rust-based; SIMD-accelerated text search. ag (silver searcher) predecessor. Used by VS Code's find feature. Can use Rust regex or --pcre2 for Perl-compatible regex. --hidden for dotfiles.

gotchas: CLI tool — invoke from code via subprocess
Different regex syntax from grep by default (Rust regex engine)
best_when: Replacement for grep in scripts needing type filtering
VS Code/Neovim powered search (default search backend)
summary: ripgrep (rg) is the fastest code search tool — 5-10x faster than grep with Rust/SIMD optimization. Respects .gitignore, Unicode-aware, -t for file types, --json for scripts. Default search engine in VS Code and Neovim. Replace grep in developer workflows for codebase search. Essential tool in developer dotfiles alongside fzf.
